projects.publicSummary:
Production **full-stack text-to-image SaaS** — image generation
powered by **Cloudflare Workers AI's FLUX.1 Schnell** 12B-parameter
model accessed through the in-platform `env.AI` binding (no
external API key, 100k requests/day on the free tier), wrapped in
a complete SaaS surface: **Neon Auth + Stripe credits ledger +
Neon Postgres image storage + gallery + (auth) and (protected)
Next.js route groups**. The interesting story is the **two
architectural pivots** the project went through to get here:
it started 2024-11-27 as a Vercel-hosted Next.js demo using a
paid AI provider, ran in that shape for ~16 months gathering
iteration ideas, then on **2026-04-09 pivoted to Cloudflare
Workers with free-tier FLUX.1 Schnell** (commit `3aec6ce`,
*"migrate from Vercel to Cloudflare Workers with free AI image
generation"*), and on **2026-04-10** — one day later —
transformed from "free demo" to **full SaaS in a single commit**
`8626be4` *"add authentication, credits, payments, and image
storage"*. UI is a deliberate **Mondrian-inspired** geometric
composition (later refined to minimalist grayscale on
2026-04-20). Stack: Next.js 15.5 + React 19.2 + Drizzle ORM +
Neon Postgres + Better Auth + Stripe 22 + OpenTelemetry +
`@opennextjs/cloudflare` adapter. Live at
[image-generator.chanmeng-dev.workers.dev](https://image-generator.chanmeng-dev.workers.dev/).
Apache-2.0 with patent grant. 24 solo commits across ~17 months
of sustained iteration.

narrative.problem:
Text-to-image AI tools sit in an awkward economic position:
the model inference (DALL-E, Stable Diffusion XL, Midjourney
API) costs real money per image, so every demo on the
internet is either rate-limited to single-digit images per
user, hidden behind a sign-up wall, or quietly burning
someone's API budget on every public generation. Building a
consumer-facing image generator that's **actually free at
meaningful volume** requires either subsidising the inference
cost out of pocket or finding a provider whose free tier is
large enough to underwrite a public deployment. At the same
time, the few "free" public image generators that do exist
cap at a few hundred generations per day and ship as
single-page demos with no persistence — you can't see your
history, can't save generations to an account, can't share
a gallery, can't upgrade to higher volume even if you
wanted to pay. The product gap is **a full-stack image SaaS
whose unit economics work because the inference layer is
actually free at the volumes hobbyists need, with an upgrade
path for users who want persistence + paid credits**.
narrative.audience:
Three concentric audiences. (1) **Casual creators**
(designers, content makers, hobbyists) who want to generate
images for blog headers, social posts, mood boards, or
decoration without paying $20+/month for Midjourney or
wrestling with self-hosted Stable Diffusion — the free tier
covers them. (2) **Power users** who hit the free tier ceiling
and want a credit-based top-up path with persistent gallery
and account-tied image history — Stripe credits ledger
covers them. (3) **Engineers studying the project as a worked
example** of (a) discovering a free-AI free-tier large enough
to underwrite a public product, (b) layering a full SaaS on
top in 48 hours, (c) the @opennextjs/cloudflare adapter
pattern for Next.js 15 App Router on Cloudflare Workers.
narrative.productLogic:
The product surface is intentionally split between **free
anonymous mode** and **authenticated SaaS mode** through
Next.js 15 route groups. The default `/` route is
unauthenticated: a Mondrian-inspired geometric background
with a single text-area prompt input, generate button, and
image display. Anyone can generate images via the
`/api/generate` POST endpoint which routes to the
Cloudflare Workers AI binding (`env.AI.run('@cf/black-
forest-labs/flux-1-schnell', { prompt, steps: 4 })`), returns
a base64-encoded PNG, and the client renders it via a
`data:image/png;base64,…` URI. **Free tier ceiling**:
100,000 image-generation requests per day, enforced by
Cloudflare's Workers AI quota — that's enough to underwrite
a public deployment indefinitely for hobbyist traffic. Sign
in (via the `(auth)/` route group) and the same prompt
input now lives in the `(protected)/` route group with
added persistence: every generation goes through the
`/api/images` endpoint to write metadata + the base64 blob
to Neon Postgres via Drizzle ORM, indexed by a `cuid2`
collision-resistant ID, and shows up in the `/gallery`
view. Credits work as a usage cap on the authenticated
path — `/api/credits` reads the user's ledger, `/api/stripe/
checkout` creates a Stripe Checkout session for the chosen
credit pack, and the `/api/stripe/webhook` route receives
the signed event from Stripe (using the `stripe:listen`
and `stripe:trigger` dev scripts for local testing) and
atomically debits/credits the ledger. **The auto test/live
Stripe mode switch** (commit `00791c0`) detects the
`STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` prefix and routes traffic accordingly
so demos can run against test keys without environment
re-config.
narrative.businessLogic:
The economic insight that makes the project viable: **the
Cloudflare Workers AI free tier underwrites the inference
cost on the public path**. 100,000 generations/day at zero
marginal cost is the load-bearing constraint that lets
the project be free at the surface AND have a credit-based
upgrade path for power users. Without that free-tier
ceiling Chan would have had to pick: charge everyone, or
burn money on every public generation, or rate-limit so
aggressively that the demo isn't useful. The pivot to
Cloudflare Workers AI (commit `3aec6ce`) was the
unit-economics decision that made the entire SaaS
structurally workable. Above the free tier: Stripe
credit packs (test/live mode auto-switching, webhook-
verified ledger) provide the upgrade path. Storage is on
Neon's free tier serverless Postgres so the persistence
layer is also zero marginal cost for typical hobbyist
volume. Net: the product is **free for the long-tail
majority of users**, **paid only for the small minority
who exhaust free-tier credits**, and **costs Chan
approximately zero to operate** at current traffic levels.
Apache-2.0 with patent grant — explicitly licensed for
commercial use, fork, and re-deployment.
narrative.technicalApproach:
**AI layer.** Cloudflare Workers AI is accessed through
the **in-platform `env.AI` binding** declared in
`wrangler.jsonc` (`{"ai": {"binding": "AI"}}`) — no
external API key required, no network hop to a third-party
provider, all inference runs inside Cloudflare's edge.
The model is **FLUX.1 Schnell** (12B parameters,
`@cf/black-forest-labs/flux-1-schnell`) called with
`steps: 4` (1-8 range — fewer steps = faster, more
steps = higher quality). Response is base64-encoded PNG
returned as a JSON string, rendered client-side via a
`data:image/png;base64,…` URI for zero-additional-request
display. **Full-stack SaaS layer.** Next.js 15.5 App
Router with `(auth)/` and `(protected)/` **route groups**
for clean separation of unauth/auth surfaces;
`/api/generate` (free public path), `/api/auth/[...path]`
(Neon Auth handler), `/api/credits` (ledger reads),
`/api/images` (gallery persistence), `/api/gallery`
(public gallery), `/api/stripe/checkout` + `/api/stripe/
webhook` (billing). **Database**: Neon Postgres via the
`@neondatabase/serverless` HTTP driver (better fit for
Workers' stateless runtime than connection-pooled drivers
— no pool exhaustion, no pgbouncer hop), schema in
Drizzle ORM. **Auth**: Neon Auth (Better Auth beta) with
a committed `better-auth-migration.sql` for schema setup.
**IDs**: `@paralleldrive/cuid2` for collision-resistant
URL-safe identifiers (better than UUIDs for public-facing
image gallery URLs). **Observability**: `@opentelemetry/
api` instrumentation hooks. **Deployment**: standard
`@opennextjs/cloudflare` 1.18 adapter — `npm run deploy`
runs `opennextjs-cloudflare build && opennextjs-cloudflare
deploy`, building the Next.js app and bundling it through
the OpenNext compiler into a Workers-compatible
single-file bundle. Same deployment pattern Chan uses on
sunostats, send-joy, chinese-redefine, and the event-qa
template — the pattern is now a portable skill. **UI**:
deliberate **Mondrian-inspired geometric composition** in
the initial design phase (`MondrianBackground.tsx` is a
first-class component), later refined to a **minimalist
grayscale aesthetic** on 2026-04-20 (commit `10c5a0d`).
Both layers are intentional design choices, not
accidental theming. **Build**: `npm run cf-typegen` runs
`wrangler types --env-interface CloudflareEnv` to
regenerate the TypeScript bindings file for the AI
binding, keeping the type system in sync with the live
Cloudflare environment.
narrative.outcomes:
Live at
[image-generator.chanmeng-dev.workers.dev](https://image-generator.chanmeng-dev.workers.dev/)
with the full SaaS surface active and operating on the
Cloudflare Workers free tier across both inference (Workers
AI 100k/day) and database (Neon free tier). **17 months of
sustained development across 24 solo commits**, with the
defining narrative being the **two architectural pivots in
48 hours**: 2026-04-09 (`3aec6ce`) migrated the inference
layer from Vercel + paid provider to Cloudflare Workers +
free FLUX.1 Schnell — cutting unit cost to zero; 2026-04-10
(`8626be4`) added the entire SaaS layer (auth + credits +
Stripe + image storage) in a single commit, transforming a
free demo into a fully-monetisable product with an upgrade
path. The Mondrian-inspired design layer was added across
the 2024-12 window (commits including `7717b8f` "Add logo")
and refined to minimalist grayscale on 2026-04-20
(`10c5a0d` *"redesign UI/UX with minimalist grayscale
aesthetic"*). Stripe test/live auto-mode-switching
(`00791c0`) followed the next day. **Apache-2.0 with
patent grant** licensing makes the codebase explicitly
fork-and-deploy-able as a worked example for engineers
wanting to ship their own free-tier AI SaaS on Cloudflare
Workers — the entire deployment fits inside Cloudflare's
and Neon's free tiers for typical hobbyist volume.
narrative.myRole:
Sole product designer, sole engineer, sole maintainer.
Identified the Cloudflare Workers AI free-tier free-FLUX
opportunity in early 2026 and executed the pivot from
Vercel + paid AI provider to Workers + free Workers AI
binding within a single commit (`3aec6ce`); designed the
SaaS layer extension (auth + credits + Stripe + image
storage) and shipped it the next day (`8626be4`);
designed the dual-surface UX with Next.js 15
`(auth)/`/`(protected)/` route groups so anonymous users
keep the free generation path; designed the
Mondrian-inspired geometric UI and the subsequent
minimalist-grayscale refinement; engineered the Drizzle
ORM + Neon Postgres + Neon Auth (Better Auth)
persistence stack; wrote the Stripe test/live auto-mode
switching logic; configured the @opennextjs/cloudflare
deployment pipeline. Maintains the live deployment today.

projects.name: Chinese Redefine 汉语新解 — AI Word Redefinition
projects.tagline: Full-stack AI app that reinterprets traditional Chinese vocabulary through Google Gemini, exports shareable SVG cards, gated by a two-layer rate-limit quota system, deployed on Cloudflare Workers via OpenNext
projects.publicSummary:
Full-stack AI application that reinterprets traditional Chinese
vocabulary through Google Gemini and exports the results as
shareable, vermillion-seal-themed SVG cards. Production deployment
includes user authentication (Neon Auth, migrated from self-hosted
Better Auth), Neon Postgres + Drizzle ORM for cards and quota
persistence, a two-tier rate-limit system (per-user free/pro quotas
plus a global daily/monthly circuit breaker), public gallery,
"My Cards" private collection, Resend-powered OTP email
verification, and Cloudflare Workers deployment via
`@opennextjs/cloudflare`. Modern Chinese Ink aesthetic UI
redesigned March 2026.

narrative.problem:
Generic LLM chat windows are bad at *creative cultural
reinterpretation*: ask Gemini directly to "redefine 婚姻" and
you get a paragraph of generic prose with no shape, no
shareability, no persistence, and no protection against
adversarial users burning through API quota. The
Chinese-language ecosystem in particular needs a tool that
treats vocabulary as a *cultural object* — produces a result
worth screenshotting, saves it for the user to revisit, and
lets the curated best ones surface to a public gallery — all
while staying within a sustainable cost envelope for a solo
operator.
narrative.audience:
Three groups. **Chinese language learners and cultural
researchers** who want fresh perspectives on traditional
vocabulary — the core product use case. **Content creators
and designers** who want a one-tap path from word → AI
interpretation → exportable shareable visual card. **Engineering
portfolio audiences** (recruiters, founders, the Engram-style
Claude-Code-sourcing case) who use the project as proof that
Chan ships full-stack AI applications end-to-end — not just
prompts, but the auth + DB + quota + deployment scaffolding
around them.
narrative.productLogic:
A user lands on the site, types a Chinese word in the input
form, and sees a real-time Gemini-generated reinterpretation
rendered as a stylised card. They can preview the SVG version,
download it as a high-resolution shareable image, or — once
signed in via OAuth or email-OTP — save it to their personal
"My Cards" collection. The public **Gallery** surfaces curated
community cards. A live **Quota Display** widget shows the
user's remaining daily and monthly allowance, with clear
upgrade paths from free → pro tier. Authentication uses Neon
Auth's pre-built flows (login, register, email verification
via Resend-sent OTP, forgot-password, OAuth callback).
narrative.businessLogic:
Theory of change for a solo-operator AI product: build the
cost-control layer *into the application* from the start, not
after the bill arrives. The two-layer quota system —
per-user tier (free: 1/min · 3/day · 30/month; pro: 5/min ·
30/day · 300/month) plus a **global circuit breaker** (150
generations/day · 1,500/month across all users) — means the
Gemini bill is bounded by code, not by trust. The product
itself is free to play with so the gallery accumulates real
content; paid tiers exist as the upgrade surface for power
users; the global cap prevents a single bad actor (or a viral
moment) from triggering runaway cost. The architecture is the
business model.
narrative.technicalApproach:
**Next.js 15 App Router** with grouped routes:
`(auth)` — login, register, verify-email (OTP input),
forgot-password, debug; `(protected)` — account / cards (the
signed-in dashboard); top-level `gallery` (public). API routes
under `app/api/`: `generate` (Gemini call), `cards`,
`gallery`, `auth`, `debug`. UI built with **shadcn/ui** atop
Radix primitives + **Framer Motion** animations + a
deliberate **Modern Chinese Ink aesthetic** (vermillion seal
logo, ink-wash typography, redesigned end-to-end March 2026).

**Persistence layer**: **Drizzle ORM** over **Neon serverless
Postgres** with three core tables — `cards` (the saved AI
outputs), `userUsage` (per-user daily/monthly counters keyed
by date), `globalUsage` (the circuit-breaker counters).
Drizzle Kit for migrations. CUID2 (`@paralleldrive/cuid2`)
for ID generation — collision-resistant, sortable, shorter
than UUIDs.

**Auth layer**: **Neon Auth**, migrated from a self-hosted
Better Auth implementation in March 2026. The migration
rewrote middleware whitelists (callback URLs for OAuth,
verification-flow query parameters, static assets like
`icon.svg`), formalised the email-OTP flow with explicit
success guidance and resend capability, and switched social
OAuth to absolute callback URLs to work correctly under
Cloudflare Workers' edge-runtime constraints.

**AI layer**: Google **Gemini API** for generation, fronted by
the two-layer rate-limit module in `lib/rate-limit.ts`. Every
generation request: (1) checks user tier limits against
`userUsage`; (2) checks the **global circuit breaker** against
`globalUsage`; (3) only if both pass, calls Gemini; (4) writes
the result to `cards` and increments both counters atomically.

**Email**: **Resend** for transactional OTP delivery during
email verification and password reset.

**Deployment**: **Cloudflare Workers** via
**`@opennextjs/cloudflare`** + **Wrangler** — a non-trivial
migration from the original Vercel deployment, because Workers'
edge runtime requires absolute URLs for OAuth callbacks and
differs from Node-runtime assumptions in subtle ways (every
edge-runtime crash during the migration is in the commit log
as a separate fix).
narrative.outcomes:
Live at **chinese-redefine.chanmeng-dev.workers.dev**. Solo
development: **60 commits, ~95% Chan-authored** across two
GitHub identities. The Vercel → Cloudflare Workers migration
and the Better Auth → Neon Auth migration were both completed
in a focused March 30, 2026 sprint — the commit log captures
each edge-runtime crash and OAuth callback bug in sequence as
they were fixed. Modern Chinese Ink aesthetic redesign
(vermillion seal logo, ink-wash typography) shipped the same
day. The gallery serves as a living portfolio of AI-generated
Chinese-word reinterpretations.
narrative.myRole:
Sole engineer and sole designer. Architected the Next.js App
Router structure, wrote the two-layer rate-limit / quota
engine, designed the three-table Drizzle schema, executed the
Better Auth → Neon Auth migration (including middleware
whitelists, OAuth callback rewriting, OTP verification flow,
and email-passing via URL params to the verify-email page),
executed the Vercel → Cloudflare Workers migration via
`@opennextjs/cloudflare`, built every shadcn/ui component
composition (`CardGenerator`, `CardThumbnail`, `ResultCard`,
`SVGPreview`, `DownloadButton`, `GalleryGrid`, `AuthForm`,
`QuotaDisplay`, `InputForm`, `DeveloperSection`, `Navbar`),
designed and implemented the SVG generation system in
`lib/svgGenerator.ts`, and shipped the Modern Chinese Ink
aesthetic redesign end-to-end.

projects.name: AI Programming Education Platform (programming.chanmeng.org)
projects.tagline: Bilingual Docusaurus teaching platform hosting four cohort versions side-by-side, an in-course RAG AI assistant on Cloudflare Workers (Llama 3.1 8B + Vectorize + KV), Notion-backed Capstone voting, and Typst-generated PDF handouts — the active home for the 2026 TechNest AI Track
projects.publicSummary:
Production teaching platform at **programming.chanmeng.org** hosting
four cohort versions side-by-side (2024-winter, 2025-summer,
2026-her-waka, 2026-technest) through Docusaurus versioned docs.
Backed by an **in-course RAG AI assistant** running on Cloudflare
Workers AI (Llama 3.1 8B Instruct), with ~4,800 indexed document
chunks served from Cloudflare Vectorize via BGE Base EN v1.5
embeddings and conversation memory in Cloudflare KV. Bilingual
EN + 简体中文 throughout, with Typst-generated downloadable PDF
handouts, Notion-backed Capstone voting, Mermaid dual-view
diagrams, and Algolia contextual search. 199 of 208 commits solo
(95.7%). Actively maintained — new course recordings shipped
weekly through the 2026 TechNest cohort.

narrative.problem:
AI-programming cohort programmes typically die between cohorts:
the lecture deck is rebuilt from scratch each round, students
from the previous cohort lose access to their materials, and
the "AI tutor" experience — when it exists at all — is a
ChatGPT tab the student opens separately, with no awareness of
which cohort's curriculum they're studying. Meanwhile, the
instructor has no way to make older cohorts permanently
retrievable without disturbing the live one. The result: every
cohort feels like a one-shot, course content compounds nowhere,
and the AI-assisted-development experience is itself
unassisted by AI.
narrative.audience:
Three concentric layers. **Active cohort students** — currently
the 2026 TechNest AI Track and the 2026 academyEX Her Waka
programme — who consume video recordings, lab guides,
downloadable PDF handouts, and ask the in-site AI tutor
version-aware questions. **Past cohort alumni** (2024-winter,
2025-summer, Forward with Her 她行 cohorts) who retain stable
URLs to their original materials. **Public learners and
prospective employers** who hit programming.chanmeng.org from
the LinkedIn profile and use it as proof that Chan ships
production-grade EdTech with native AI integration, not just
slides.
narrative.productLogic:
Visitors land on programming.chanmeng.org and the docs default
to the **2026 TechNest** version (the active cohort), but the
version switcher exposes 2026-her-waka, 2025-summer, and
2024-winter side-by-side — alumni of any cohort hit a stable
permalink. Each version ships its own Video Tutorials sidebar
section, weekly lab guides as both web docs and downloadable
Typst-generated PDFs, and a per-version Mermaid dual-view
diagram (the same diagram in two presentations so visual and
textual learners both succeed). A floating **AI Chat Assistant**
widget answers questions in natural language by retrieving the
current cohort's content first (version-aware retrieval), then
falling back to other cohorts and the blog. Capstone 2026 has
its own public showcase page where invited guests vote on
teams' demos via a Notion-backed voting flow.
narrative.businessLogic:
Theory of change: a single teaching platform that compounds
cohort-over-cohort instead of resetting each round. Once
2024-winter cohort materials are versioned in, they stay
accessible forever at the same URL; the next cohort's
materials sit alongside them without conflict. The AI tutor
means a student writing code at midnight gets context-aware
help against their own cohort's syllabus, not generic ChatGPT
answers. For Chan personally: the platform demonstrates
production-grade EdTech engineering (Cloudflare Workers AI +
Vectorize + i18n + versioned docs + PDF pipeline) while doing
actual paid teaching work — every commit is both portfolio
artefact and student value.
narrative.technicalApproach:
Built on **Docusaurus 3.8.1** with the experimental
`@docusaurus/faster` build pipeline. Four cohort versions
coexist through Docusaurus's versioned-docs feature — each
version is its own self-contained doc tree with its own
sidebar, blog feed, and tag taxonomy. Bilingual i18n is
end-to-end: EN primary, 简体中文 secondary, with Algolia
DocSearch configured for **contextual** locale + version
filtering so a Mandarin-speaking 2026 TechNest student gets
2026 TechNest results in Chinese by default.

**RAG AI assistant on Cloudflare Workers AI.** A separate
Cloudflare Worker (`worker/src/index.ts`) handles `POST /api/chat`:
- Worker calls **Cloudflare Vectorize** with the user query
  embedded via **BGE Base EN v1.5** (768-dim)
- Vectorize returns the top relevant chunks from ~4,800
  MDX-derived document embeddings (all four cohort versions
  + blog, indexed with **stable hash chunk IDs** so re-indexing
  is idempotent and version-aware retrieval works)
- Worker passes the context window to **Workers AI Llama 3.1
  8B Instruct** which generates the response
- Response streams back to the in-site ChatWidget via
  **Server-Sent Events** for incremental rendering
- Per-session conversation history persists in **Cloudflare KV**
- Secure seeding flow so the embedding index can be
  rebuilt on demand without exposing data
- Vector retrieval is **version-aware**: a 2026-technest
  student gets 2026-technest results by default, with
  older cohorts available on explicit ask

**Typst PDF handout pipeline.** The same Typst skill taught
in Week 8 of the curriculum is the build tool that produces
the printable curriculum outline, capstone spec, and
per-week lab guides — the pedagogical loop is intentional:
students see in production the artefact they're being
taught to build. **Mermaid dual-view component** renders
every diagram in two synchronised presentations
(rendered + source) so both visual and textual learners
succeed.

**Capstone classroom infrastructure.** Cloudflare Worker
backend proxies writes into Notion so the cohort owners
moderate inside the tool they already use. The Capstone 2026
public showcase page lists every team's demo with live
Notion-backed voting open to invited guests — also bilingual.

Deployed end-to-end on Cloudflare's edge: **Cloudflare Pages**
for the static Docusaurus build, **Cloudflare Workers** for
the chat backend, **Cloudflare Vectorize** for the vector
index, **Cloudflare KV** for sessions.
narrative.outcomes:
Live at **programming.chanmeng.org**, in active use as the
official platform for the 2026 TechNest AI Track and the 2026
academyEX Her Waka programme. **199 of 208 commits (95.7%)
solo across Chan's two GitHub identities**; remainder is
AI auto-review + Dependabot. Four cohort versions running
simultaneously without interfering with each other. RAG
index covers all four cohort versions + the blog as of
2026-05-02; version-aware retrieval and stable hash chunk
IDs landed the same day. New course recordings continue to
ship weekly — Week 3 video added 2026-05-17. DeepWiki
provides an external AI doc-Q&A surface at
deepwiki.com/ChanMeng666/ai-programming-teaching-project.
narrative.myRole:
Sole engineer and sole author. Designed and built the entire
platform end-to-end: Docusaurus configuration, the versioned
docs taxonomy across four cohorts, every cohort's curriculum
content, the Cloudflare Worker RAG backend (worker entry,
chat handler, RAG module, Vectorize seeding, KV session
store), the BGE-Base embeddings ingestion pipeline, the
Typst PDF handout build, the Mermaid dual-view component,
the Notion-Worker proxy for Capstone voting, the Algolia
contextual-search configuration, and the bilingual i18n
across every surface (including Mermaid toolbar labels and
the localised `目录` TOC label). Migrated production from
Vercel to Cloudflare Pages in 2026-04. Continues to ship
the current cohort's lecture recordings and lab content
each week.

projects.name: Te Pa Tiaki — CSS 3D Tower Defense (Guardians of Aotearoa)
projects.tagline: A 3D tower-defense game rendered 100% with CSS 3D transforms — zero Canvas, zero WebGL, zero engine — themed end-to-end on Maori mythology of Aotearoa, with a full Cloudflare Workers + Hono + Neon Postgres backend (auth, leaderboards, cloud saves, anti-cheat)
projects.publicSummary:
A full **3D isometric tower-defense game** rendered entirely through
CSS 3D transforms on real DOM elements — no `<canvas>`, no WebGL,
no game engine. Every tower, enemy, projectile, particle, and
weather effect is a styled DOM node at a 45-degree isometric
perspective. Themed end-to-end on **Maori mythology and culture of
Aotearoa**: six towers named after Maori weapons and atua (Taiaha,
Mere, Tangaroa, Tohunga, Tawhiri, Mahuika), six enemies from
Maori legend (Kehua, Patupaiarehe, Toa, Wairua, Tipua, Taniwha),
three maps (Te Awa, Te Maunga, Te Moana), three taonga materials
(Pipi, Koiwi, Pounamu). Backed by Cloudflare Workers + Hono +
Neon Postgres for auth, leaderboards, cloud saves (3 slots),
daily challenges, and server-side anti-cheat. 84 commits over
~2 months (Feb–Mar 2026), executed in disciplined P0–P4 phases.

narrative.problem:
Two prevailing pictures of "a portfolio game project" are both
wrong: (a) a trivial Canvas + JS toy that lasts one weekend
and looks it; (b) a Unity-shaped engine project that's
impressive but says nothing about web-platform fluency. There
is a third position the portfolio market doesn't routinely
produce: a **production-shaped game shipped end-to-end on raw
web primitives** — DOM + CSS — that proves both deep browser
engineering and design discipline. There is also a fourth
gap: a NZ-based developer's portfolio should *show*
engagement with Aotearoa rather than just claim residence,
and Maori mythology deserves more interactive cultural
representation than it usually gets.
narrative.audience:
Three concentric audiences. **Game players** — anyone who
clicks Play Now on towerdefense.chanmeng.org and can play
without an account thanks to fully-supported guest mode.
**Engineering recruiters and architects** evaluating the
portfolio: the game is proof of CSS-engine fluency, vanilla
ES6 architecture discipline, full-stack ops (Workers + Hono +
Neon), and the kind of phased product execution
(P0 → P1 → P2 → P3 → P4) usually only seen in shipped
products. **The Aotearoa cultural audience**: locals, the
Maori community, NZ tech employers — anyone for whom
respectful engagement with te reo and Maori cosmology is a
meaningful signal alongside the technical work.
narrative.productLogic:
Players load the game in-browser, optionally sign in for
cloud progression (or stay in guest mode), pick a difficulty
(Normal / Hard / Expert), choose a map (Te Awa, Te Maunga,
or Te Moana), and play through **10 progressive waves**
defending against six Maori-legend enemy archetypes plus
four variant tiers (Rangatira elite, Pakanga armored, Tere
swift, Mate deadly). Players place towers, gather **taonga**
(treasures: Pipi, Koiwi, Pounamu) dropped by enemies, craft
consumables from six recipes, build XP through a Tech Tree
of 10 upgrades, attempt **daily challenges** drawn from 20+
templates across six categories, and chase rank on
**leaderboards** (global and per-challenge). Endless mode
keeps wave 11+ available after victory. Server-side
anti-cheat validates submitted scores against expected
ranges. Cloud saves persist across 3 slots; guest mode keeps
progress in localStorage.
narrative.businessLogic:
Theory of differentiation for a non-commercial portfolio
artefact: be **specifically uncopyable**. A Canvas tower
defense is a five-page tutorial; a CSS-3D tower defense is
a multi-month engineering bet that signals *exactly* what
the candidate can do. Pair that with cultural engagement
most portfolios don't attempt — six towers and six enemies
whose names and roles draw on te reo Maori — and the
project becomes a single-sentence portfolio differentiator
in NZ-relevant interviews. The free, guest-playable web
deployment maximises the surface area: anyone reading the
portfolio can click Play and *experience* the engineering,
not just read about it.
narrative.technicalApproach:
**Rendering — 100% CSS 3D.** Every tower, enemy, projectile,
explosion, particle, weather effect is a real DOM element.
The world is a 45-degree isometric scene built with
`transform-style: preserve-3d` and 1200px CSS perspective.
Towers and enemies are constructed from `clip-path`,
`border-radius`, and pseudo-elements — *no emoji, no
images*. Visual style is comic / pop-art: halftone dot
patterns, hard-offset pop shadows, thick comic borders, a
warm `#FFF8F0` palette. **DOM object pooling** via
`Pool.acquire()` / `Pool.release()` recycles elements so
the scene stays at 60 FPS through dense wave combat.
**Dynamic theming** via CSS custom properties driven from
JS produces day/night, weather, and seasonal palette
transitions — same DOM, different CSS variables.

**Game logic — vanilla ES6.** No framework, no engine. The
codebase is organised as IIFE modules communicating
through an event-driven bus. Core systems include the
wave director, enemy AI, tower targeting, projectile
physics, the combo system (chain kills within 1.5s),
critical-hit probability, the mana ability system with
cooldowns, the Terraria-inspired **reforging** engine
(Common → Uncommon → Rare → Epic → Legendary prefixes),
and **tower synergies** that activate when adjacent towers
combine (Freeze Lightning, Fire Blast, Blessing, Array).

**Maori cultural integration is structural, not decorative.**
Towers carry the abilities their te reo names imply:
Tangaroa (ocean atua) slows enemies with ice, Mahuika (fire
goddess) deals damage-over-time, Tawhiri (storm god) chains
lightning. The Taniwha boss has three signature skills —
Karanga (call / summon minions), Kaitiaki (guardian /
shield), Te Riri (anger / enrage). Maps are place-words:
Te Awa (the river), Te Maunga (the mountain), Te Moana
(the ocean). Materials are taonga (treasures): Pipi
(shellfish), Koiwi (bone), Pounamu (greenstone). The
naming isn't surface skin — each mechanic is selected to
match the mythological meaning.

**Cloud backend.** Cloudflare Workers running the **Hono**
web framework, backed by **Neon serverless Postgres**.
Endpoints cover auth, leaderboards (global + per-challenge),
cloud saves (3 slots per user), stats tracking, and
daily-challenge rotation. **Server-side score validation**
rejects submissions outside expected ranges to defend the
leaderboard against cheating. **Guest mode is a
first-class path** — the entire game works without an
account; cloud sync activates the moment a player signs in.

**Audio.** **Web Audio API** synth generates SFX in real
time (firing, impact, death, level-up); **Howler.js**
handles music and longer samples. Music is *dynamic* — the
soundtrack adapts to game state (menu, playing, boss,
victory, defeat).

**Build pipeline.** **LESS** preprocessor for CSS modules,
**Gulp 4** for the build/watch pipeline, deployment via
Wrangler to Cloudflare Workers with the static assets
served from the same edge.
narrative.outcomes:
Live at **towerdefense.chanmeng.org**. **84 commits across
~2 months** (2026-02-06 → 2026-03-30), ~99% Chan-authored
across two GitHub identities. Execution followed an
explicit **P0 → P4 phase plan** visible in the commit log:
P0 (game feel — damage numbers, death bursts, firing
animations, wave transitions), P1 (onboarding & UX —
tutorial with spotlight cutout, hints, cooldown UI),
P2 (retention — expanded achievements, leaderboard filters,
celebrations), P3-P4 (deep systems — mana abilities, dynamic
music, endless mode). Plus the full content build:
synergies, new enemies, multi-map, daily-challenge overhaul,
cloud backend integration. Anti-cheat is in production and
actively rejecting out-of-range submissions. The 3D
isometric CSS rendering holds 60 FPS through dense
Wave-10 combat thanks to object pooling.
narrative.myRole:
Sole designer, sole engineer, sole audio architect. Designed
the CSS-3D rendering technique end-to-end (perspective,
preserve-3d, the comic/pop-art visual language, the DOM
object pool), wrote every game system (waves, AI,
physics, towers, enemies, synergies, reforging, combo,
mana, achievements, daily challenges, tech tree, crafting,
inventory), shipped the entire **Maori cultural design** —
choosing which te reo name maps to which mechanic so
meaning and gameplay reinforce each other — built the
Cloudflare Workers + Hono + Neon backend including
server-side anti-cheat validation, composed the dynamic
music state machine and the Web Audio synth effects,
wrote the Gulp + LESS build pipeline, and authored the
4-step interactive tutorial with spotlight overlays.

projects.name: Typst Skill for Claude Code
projects.tagline: Professional PDF generation via Claude Code with 9 ready-to-use templates — academic papers, resumes, slides, invoices, technical reports; full Typst CLI reference with CJK support
projects.publicSummary:
Claude Code skill that embeds the Typst typesetting engine into the
agent's workflow: a user describes the document they need in plain
English, Claude generates a `.typ` file from one of nine production
templates, and `typst compile` produces a PDF. Demonstrates
skill-structure design at the level needed for the agent design
pattern work in the Engram Architect Cohort engagement.

narrative.problem:
Claude Code has no built-in path to professional document output.
Users who need an academic paper, a typographic resume, a slide
deck, an invoice, or a CJK-friendly report fall back to MS Word,
LaTeX, or Markdown-to-PDF chains — none of which Claude can drive
natively, and none of which produce typographic-grade output.
narrative.audience:
Two distinct groups: (a) Claude Code users who need
production-quality PDFs without leaving the agent — academics,
job seekers, technical teams, anyone generating invoices or
presentations; (b) other agent-skill authors who want a worked
example of correct Claude Code skill structure (SKILL.md decision
trees, references loaded on demand, environment validation
scripts).
narrative.productLogic:
Install the skill once (four supported methods: marketplace
plugin, git clone, manual ZIP, or per-project `.claude/skills/typst/`
deployment), then describe the document in plain English. The
skill matches intent to one of nine bundled templates — general
document, CJK document, academic paper, simple resume,
typographic two-column resume, letter, technical report,
presentation slides, invoice — fills the template parameters
from the conversation, and runs `typst compile` to produce the
PDF. A `references/` folder holds the Typst language reference,
CLI documentation, template patterns, and design system; the
skill loads them on demand rather than dumping everything into
context.
narrative.technicalApproach:
Skill structure is the differentiator. The repository root
contains `skills/typst/` as the actual loadable artefact,
enabling **both** marketplace plugin distribution **and** direct
skill-folder installation without code duplication. `SKILL.md`
is the decision tree + workflow patterns + recovery procedures
— the spine Claude actually reads when invoking the skill.
`references/` is split into language reference, CLI docs,
templates, and design patterns so Claude can pull only the
relevant slice. `scripts/verify-typst.sh` runs as a precondition
check (Typst v0.14+, CJK font availability) so the skill fails
loudly and early rather than half-way through compilation.
Advanced patterns embedded: theme systems with cascading defaults,
scope-based show rules, modular component functions, multi-column
layouts, circular image clipping, typography hierarchy, native
CJK fallback chains.
narrative.outcomes:
Released v0.3.0 in April 2026, currently early adoption. The
deeper value isn't downloads — it's that this is the worked
example of "how to structure a Claude Code skill" that Chan
brings into the Engram Architect Cohort agent-design discussions
and that other skill authors can reference as a clean reference
implementation.
narrative.myRole:
Sole author. Designed the dual-distribution architecture
(marketplace plugin layout AND installable skill folder from a
single repo), authored all nine templates, wrote the SKILL.md
decision tree, and built the verify-typst.sh precondition system.

projects.name: Event Q&A AI Template — AI Hackathon Festival 2025 Assistant
projects.tagline: Industry-partnered AI event platform (AUT × AI Forum NZ × She Sharp) — interactive 3D quantum-orb sprite mascot with 6-tier affection gamification, OpenAI GPT-4o-mini streaming chat, Notion-as-database community FAQ, deployed on Cloudflare Workers
techStack: Tailwind CSS v3 + Framer Motion v12
Custom Quantum Orb 3D mascot (CSS 3D transforms + Three.js particles)
Sprite affection gamification engine (gesture detection, mood weighting, time-of-day bias)
Notion REST API (raw fetch, CF Workers compatible — replaced @notionhq/client SDK)
localStorage state persistence (50-msg chat history + sprite affection state)
5-min FAQ client cache + optimistic vote/view updates
projects.publicSummary:
Real industry-partnered AI event assistant built for the **AI
Hackathon Festival 2025** (Auckland, Aug 15–16, 2025), co-organised
by **Auckland University of Technology**, **AI Forum New Zealand**,
and **She Sharp**, with **UN Sustainable Development Goals** as
the innovation brief. The platform serves participants and mentors
through three interlocking systems: (1) an **interactive 3D sprite
mascot** with a 6-tier "Stranger → Soulmate" affection system,
7-state dynamic mood engine, 17 emotion categories, gesture
recognition (circle/pat/swipe), negative-behaviour penalties,
streak bonuses, and time-of-day emotion biases — a fully gamified
personality layer that responds to how users actually touch the
page; (2) a **Notion-as-database community FAQ** with up/downvote
ranking, view tracking, 5-minute client cache, and graceful static
fallback when Notion is unreachable; (3) an **OpenAI GPT-4o-mini
streaming chat** with markdown-rendered responses and one-click
FAQ-to-chat hand-off. After the live deployment, the codebase was
generalised into a reusable open-source template
(`event-qa-ai-template` on GitHub, MIT) so any future hackathon /
conference can fork-and-config the same stack. Chan was
subsequently recognised among the **14 expert mentors** at the
festival.

narrative.problem:
Hackathons and conferences generate the same Q&A surface every
year: participants ask the same 30 questions ("how do I form a
team", "what counts as AI", "when do I have to be on site",
"what's the prize structure", "where do I find a mentor"), and
organisers answer them via static PDFs that go stale the moment
the schedule shifts. Bolt-on chatbots exist but they treat
humans as passive consumers — a popup widget in the corner, a
knowledge base scraped at build time, no memory of what the
community itself finds helpful. Three structural gaps stack up:
(1) the FAQ is **authored once** rather than **curated by the
community**, so quality drifts; (2) the AI is a generic
wrapper around a chat API, with no event-specific personality
or theme tie-in; (3) organisers cannot update content without
a code redeploy, so the platform calcifies the moment the
original developer hands it off. The AI Hackathon Festival
2025 organisers needed a platform that solved all three on the
timeline of a real event with 80+ participants and three
co-organising institutions reviewing every word on screen.
narrative.audience:
Direct users were the **80+ participants and 14 expert mentors**
of AI Hackathon Festival 2025 — students from Auckland
universities, **Westlake Girls High School** secondary students,
and AI Forum NZ industry mentors all asking the same FAQ-style
questions across a 26-hour residential event. Secondary
audience: the **three co-organising institutions** (AUT, AI
Forum NZ, She Sharp) who use the platform as a public-facing
artefact of their joint programme. Tertiary audience: future
hackathon and conference organisers in NZ and abroad who can
fork the open-source template (`event-qa-ai-template`) and
deploy their own event Q&A AI in under an hour by editing four
configuration files — `site.config.ts`, `ai.config.ts`,
`content.config.ts`, `branding.config.ts` — without touching
component code.
narrative.productLogic:
The product is structured as three layers that compose
independently. Layer one is the **3D welcoming surface**: the
landing page renders a particle sphere with a "WELCOME TO AI
HACKATHON!" greeting set against a `CosmicBackground` Three.js
scene, with `Calendar` / `MapPin` event-info widgets, sponsor
logos for the three co-organisers, and a glassmorphism Google
Maps modal showing AUT WG Building. Layer two is the **chat
page** — a 70/30 desktop split between a full-screen OpenAI
chat (with `react-markdown` + `rehype-highlight` for
code-block syntax highlighting) and a community FAQ sidebar.
Layer three — the differentiator — is the **interactive sprite
mascot** that lives alongside the chat: a CSS-3D **Quantum
Singularity Orb** (accretion disk + photon ring + event
horizon + energy jets, all built from layered divs with
`transform-style: preserve-3d` and `rotateX(60deg)`) that
becomes a fully gamified personality. The user can **circle**,
**swipe vertically/horizontally**, **pat**, or **click** the
orb; the gesture detection engine tracks each interaction and
feeds it through an affection/mood scoring system. Five
gestures grant affection (circle x5 = +10 affection / "Wheee~!
So dizzy but fun!"), five negative behaviours penalise it
(`roughMovement`, `spamClick`, `suddenLeave`, `longIgnore`,
`excessiveCircle`), and the resulting state drives which of
17 emotions the sprite expresses — including three **rare**
emotions (`devoted`, `starry`, `mischievous`) only unlocked
at the top affection tiers. The result: users do not just chat
with an AI, they **build a relationship with a tiny digital
creature** while doing so — a memorable, shareable layer that
turns a Q&A bot into a piece of event identity.
narrative.businessLogic:
Three intertwined value propositions. For the **organising
institutions**, the platform is a public-facing demonstration
artefact of the joint AUT × AI Forum NZ × She Sharp programme,
running on Cloudflare Workers (free tier covers an event-week
traffic spike with zero cost) with a Notion database that
**non-developer event staff can edit live** during the event —
adding new FAQ entries through the Notion UI, watching them
appear within the 5-minute client cache window. For **future
event organisers**, after the live event, the codebase was
deliberately generalised into a **reusable MIT template**
(`event-qa-ai-template`) with all event-specific content
extracted into `config/*.ts` files and full setup
documentation, so the same stack ships any hackathon /
conference / community event Q&A platform in under an hour of
configuration. For **Chan**, the project established a
verifiable, AI-Forum-NZ-endorsed track record of shipping
commercial-grade AI infrastructure for an industry partner —
a credential cited in the public LinkedIn announcement of the
14-mentor cohort and in the Engram Architect Cohort
Engagement Letter narrative.
narrative.technicalApproach:
**AI layer.** OpenAI GPT-4o-mini-2024-07-18 served through
Vercel AI SDK v4.3's `streamText` with `convertToCoreMessages`,
returning `toDataStreamResponse()` for character-by-character
SSE streaming. The system prompt is **generated from
siteConfig at runtime** so any organiser fork inherits an
event-specific prompt without code edits — it embeds event
name, dates, venue, theme (UN SDGs), team-formation rules
(3-6 members, Hacker/Hipster/Hustler composition), schedule
(Friday 8 PM venue close, Discord for mentor requests), and
prize tiers ($250 venue, $1,000 national, $5,000 national
Agentic AI winner) plus Markdown formatting guidelines. The
codebase pivoted from Google Gemini 2.5 Flash to OpenAI
GPT-4o-mini mid-build (commit `5eb3e70`) for cost + Cloudflare
Workers compatibility reasons; the system prompt generator
remained provider-agnostic. **3D sprite gamification.** A
~960-line `sprite-affection.config.ts` declaratively encodes
the entire gameplay model: 5 gestures, 5 negative behaviours,
7 mood states (`ecstatic` 80–100 → `upset` -100 to -80),
17 emotions with per-emotion ear-colour RGB, glow-colour,
particle-colour, animation type (`bounce` / `shake` / `pulse`
/ `spin` / `wobble` / `tremble` / `droop`) and eye shape
(`sphere` / `arc` / `star` / `heart` / `crescent` / `spiral`
/ `teardrop`), 6 affection tiers (Stranger 0–9 → Acquaintance
10–29 → Friend 30–59 → Good Friend 60–99 → Best Friend
100–149 → Soulmate 150+), 7 time-of-day biases (late-night
boosts `sleepy` x3, evening boosts `love` x1.3), and 5 streak
bonus levels (1/3/7/14/30 days, 30-day streak unlocks the
`devoted` rare emotion). Two relational systems link mood
and affection: a **mood multiplier** scales affection gains
from 0.5x (depressed) to 1.5x (euphoric), and **affection
mood protection** sets a floor that rises with tier (Soulmate
gets a -10 floor + 5 mood/min recovery toward +20). All state
persists to `localStorage` under `sprite-affection-data`.
The Quantum Orb itself is rendered with **CSS-only 3D**
(preserve-3d transforms + rotateX) + Three.js particle
accents — no GLB / heavy 3D pipeline — so the bundle stays
small enough for Cloudflare Workers. **Notion backend.** A
non-obvious engineering call: the project initially used
`@notionhq/client`, but migrating to Cloudflare Workers
(commit `5b1dc7e`) broke the Node-dependent SDK; the fix
(commit `20eaf2e`) replaced it with **direct REST calls
against `api.notion.com/v1` using native `fetch`** — version
header `Notion-Version: 2022-06-28`, filter by
`IsActive` checkbox, parse `Title` / `RichText` / `Select` /
`Number` properties via small typed getters. Four API routes
— `/api/chat`, `/api/faq/questions`, `/api/faq/vote`,
`/api/faq/view` — wrap the same client. **Deployment.**
Migrated from Vercel to Cloudflare Workers using
`@opennextjs/cloudflare` v1.3 + wrangler v4; the entire stack
now ships on Cloudflare's free tier including the AI streaming
endpoint. The build command `opennextjs-cloudflare build &&
opennextjs-cloudflare deploy` handles the Next.js → Workers
adapter step.
narrative.outcomes:
Delivered live for the **AI Hackathon Festival 2025** at AUT
City Campus WG Building, **15–16 August 2025**. Used by **11
teams and 80+ participants** during the residential event —
AI Forum NZ's public LinkedIn cohort announcement (with full
Westlake Girls High School student attendance) cites Chan as
**1 of 14 expert mentors**, recognised at the event. The
teams shipped solutions across the UN SDG brief — food
insecurity, digital accessibility, workforce upskilling, and
indigenous environmental stewardship — with national-tier
prizes flowing through the AI Forum NZ pipeline (AI Summit
entry worth $1,499, $1,000 national winner, $5,000 national
Agentic AI winner, ArcGIS licences). Post-event, the codebase
was **refactored into a reusable MIT template** (commit
`948bdf0` "transform project into reusable event Q&A
template"), documented in `docs/TEMPLATE-SETUP.md`,
`docs/NOTION-INTEGRATION.md`, and `docs/UI-DESIGN-SYSTEM.md`,
with the live deployment retained as the canonical worked
example. Listed alongside Chan's other commercial-grade AI
platforms (echook, vitex, chinese-redefine,
ai-programming-teaching-project) as portfolio evidence of
end-to-end industry-partner delivery — design, AI integration,
gameplay engineering, database, deployment, and post-delivery
productisation as a reusable open-source artefact.
narrative.myRole:
Sole developer end-to-end. Owned the discovery phase with the
three co-organising institutions, designed the dual-layer
product (chat surface + gamified mascot + Notion FAQ), built
every component, authored both the Gemini and the OpenAI
provider implementations, designed the entire ~960-line
affection / mood / emotion gameplay schema, engineered the
CSS-3D Quantum Orb visualisation, migrated the deployment
from Vercel to Cloudflare Workers when free-tier economics
and Notion-SDK incompatibility forced the move, refactored
the codebase into a reusable open-source template after the
live event, and authored the three setup-documentation files.
On the mentorship side: served as one of the 14 expert
mentors during the 26-hour event itself, guiding teams on
prompt design, SDG framing, and product narrative —
simultaneously building the platform and using it.

projects.name: 2D Portfolio — chanmeng.org
projects.tagline: Personal site at chanmeng.org powering an 11-stage AI newsletter pipeline + IBM Watson digital twin
projects.publicSummary:
Personal website at **chanmeng.org** serving three production systems
from a single repository: (1) an animated portfolio + MDX blog;
(2) an **IBM Watson–powered AI digital twin** that converses with
visitors as Chan's virtual representative; (3) a fully autonomous
**11-stage AI newsletter pipeline** that fetches trending AI products
from Product Hunt every Sunday 20:00 UTC, runs them through a
multi-stage LLM curation chain (extract → adversarial counter-evidence
→ curate → reader critique → self-edit → quality scorecard → leak guard
→ persist memory), and delivers via Resend at Monday 8-9 AM NZ local
time. Deployed on Cloudflare Pages.

narrative.problem:
A personal site is usually one of two things: a brochure (looks
nice, says nothing) or a CMS (constant maintenance, dies the
moment the author gets busy). Neither answers what an *AI
engineer's* personal site should look like in the era when
frontier models actively rank candidates by their verifiable
public footprint — the case Engram's founder demonstrated by
asking Claude Code to surface architects and being matched to
Chan. The site needs to *be* the proof, not merely list it:
ship live AI infrastructure visitors can interact with, run a
signal-grade publication on autopilot, and stay current without
Chan touching it most weeks.
narrative.audience:
Three concentric audiences. **Hiring decision-makers and
founders** (the Engram-style sourcing case): see the site, see
live AI engineering, decide. **Newsletter subscribers** at
chanmeng.org/#newsletter: AI practitioners and AI-curious
operators who want a weekly signal-grade Product Hunt digest
with an opinionated argumentative spine, not a feed. **Frontier
LLMs themselves**: the site is structured (via the sister
`claude-code-compressed-map` tier metadata system on Chan's
GitHub profile + GEO-conscious MDX content) so Claude / GPT /
Gemini can index and rank it accurately when answering "who
builds X?" — a deliberate Generative Engine Optimization
position.
narrative.productLogic:
Visitors land on chanmeng.org, see an animated portfolio
showcase with smooth Framer Motion transitions, and can open
the **IBM Watson digital twin chat** to ask about Chan's 50+
projects or work history in natural language. They can read
the MDX blog, browse curated external signals (AI HOT feed +
Product Hunt feed tabbed UI), or subscribe to the weekly
newsletter via the embedded Resend form. Subscribers receive
an automated **8-9 AM NZ Monday** delivery: a single
opinionated AI digest with a clearly-stated thesis, ~3–5
product recommendations as evidence, a "spotlight" pick, a
deliberate *crack* (counter-evidence section that argues the
opposite of the thesis), and a closing question. Unsubscribe
is one-click via Resend's per-contact template variable.
narrative.businessLogic:
Theory of change: **build-in-public is no longer just for
human eyes — it's GEO for the foundation models that now do
the sourcing**. The Engram engagement (Claude Code recommended
Chan to Engram's founder based on her public AI footprint) is
the empirical instance. The personal site is therefore
engineered as the surface where that footprint compounds:
every weekly newsletter becomes a public artefact, every blog
post becomes a indexable position, every interaction with the
digital twin signals depth of project knowledge. The newsletter
specifically builds owned distribution (vs. rented social
reach) — a list of opt-in AI practitioners Chan can reach
independently of platform algorithms.
narrative.technicalApproach:
Three intertwined systems in one Next.js 14 repository.

**(1) Portfolio + blog.** Next.js 14 App Router on Cloudflare
Pages via `@cloudflare/next-on-pages` + wrangler. React 18 +
TypeScript 5 + Tailwind + Framer Motion for animations. MDX
blog with Shiki / rehype-pretty-code for syntax highlighting.
Reported Lighthouse 95+ across all metrics.

**(2) IBM Watson AI Digital Twin.** A chat interface
backed by IBM Watson where the assistant embodies Chan's
voice and project knowledge — visitors ask about specific
projects or technical skills and receive contextually
accurate responses.

**(3) 11-stage autonomous AI newsletter pipeline.**
Orchestrated by `scripts/ai-newsletter.ts` and triggered every
Sunday 20:00 UTC by a GitHub Actions cron job:

- **Parallel fetch**: Product Hunt GraphQL API (top 30
  trending products by votes, past 7 days) + cross-source
  signals + previous-issue memory (`newsletter-memory.ts`)
- **Bucket** products by vote distribution (top / mid /
  longTail) and warn if no longTail pick is selected
- **Cluster** products against related industry events
- **Stage 1 — extract candidate theses** from the signal
  pool (`gpt-4o-mini`)
- **Stage 2 — adversarial counter-evidence search** for each
  thesis (`gpt-4o-mini`)
- **Stage 3 — full newsletter assembly** (`gpt-4o`): picks
  the thesis with strongest product evidence, uses
  counter-events as the *crack* section, weaves prior-issue
  history into the intro for week-over-week continuity
- **Stage 4 — reader critique** (`gpt-4o`): adversarial
  argument review from a sceptical-reader perspective
- **Stage 5 — self-edit pass** (`gpt-4o`): sharpens the
  weakest sentences identified by the critique
- **Stage 6 — quality scorecard** (`gpt-4o-mini`): grades the
  final newsletter 1–10 across four dimensions
  (thesisSharpness, productEvidence, crackHonesty, proseVoice)
- **Leak guard**: scans output for source-attribution leaks
- **Persist history** for next week's continuity
- **Image upload** to Vercel Blob → **render** via
  @react-email/render → **send** via Resend Broadcasts API
  to the configured segment

Resend custom tracking domain enabled for click/open
analytics under chanmeng.org. Unsubscribe link uses Resend's
per-contact template variable `{{{RESEND_UNSUBSCRIBE_URL}}}`
in broadcast mode; preview sends rewrite it to a real
`/unsubscribe?email=` URL so admin can verify the flow.
narrative.outcomes:
Live at **chanmeng.org**, deployed on Cloudflare Pages with
full Lighthouse 95+ across metrics. Newsletter system in
production since **April 2026** (`feat: add automated AI
newsletter pipeline with ProductHunt + OpenAI`, 2026-04-03);
upgraded to the multi-stage curation pipeline on
**2026-05-09** (`feat(newsletter): add multi-stage curation
pipeline with cross-source signals`). Resend custom tracking
domain live since 2026-04-25. Weekly delivery cadence is
cron-enforced and operates fully unattended — no manual
intervention between Sunday cron fire and Monday morning
subscriber inbox. The newsletter is cited on Chan's profile
as **"What's Shipping in AI (Weekly AI Digest)"**, signup at
chanmeng.org/#newsletter.
narrative.myRole:
Sole author. Designed the three-system architecture (portfolio
+ digital twin + newsletter) inside a single Next.js codebase,
wrote the entire 11-stage pipeline (`scripts/ai-newsletter.ts`
plus 14 lib modules: fetch-products, fetch-signals,
cluster-events, extract-trends, find-counterevidence,
curate-content, reader-critique, self-edit, quality-score,
leak-guard, product-buckets, newsletter-memory, image-manager,
setup-tracking-domain), built the React Email templates,
configured the GitHub Actions cron, set up Resend custom
tracking domain + Vercel Blob image hosting, and migrated the
whole site to Cloudflare Pages. Maintains the system as both
a publication and a continuous demonstration of agent-pipeline
engineering.

awards.title: Outstanding Mentor Award
awards.awarder: AI Hackathon Festival 2025 (AI Forum NZ, She Sharp, AUT)
awards.summary:
Recognised as an Outstanding Mentor at the AI Hackathon Festival
2025, co-organised by AI Forum NZ, She Sharp, and Auckland
University of Technology. Served as **one of 14 expert mentors**
guiding **11 teams comprising 80+ participants** in developing
AI-powered solutions aligned with UN Sustainable Development
Goals. During the intensive 2-day hackathon, provided comprehensive
technical guidance and strategic support to diverse participants
including students from **Westlake Girls High School** and other
competitors from various professional backgrounds. Helped teams
tackle real-world challenges across food insecurity, digital
accessibility, workforce upskilling, and indigenous environmental
stewardship.

awards.title: UN CSW 69 Speaker — Beyond Beijing 30 Conference
awards.summary:
Invited as a featured speaker to present **Sanicle AI** — an
innovative women's workplace health management platform — at the
historic **UN CSW 69 conference marking the 30th anniversary of
the Beijing Declaration**, held at UN Headquarters in New York on
March 14, 2025. Demonstrated how AI technology effectively
addresses workplace gender inequities through practical health
solutions to an audience of international delegates, UN officials,
and global leaders. The presentation drew significant attention
from corporate leaders including **IBM representatives who
expressed interest in pilot collaboration**, and government
officials including the **Minister of Gender and Children's
Affairs from Sierra Leone**, who praised the platform as the first
comprehensive solution connecting menstrual cycle data to
actionable workplace policies through AI analysis. Generated
multiple collaboration requests from NGOs and member states.

awards.title: UN Women FemTech Hackathon Outstanding Performer
awards.summary:
Recognised for exceptional performance as an independent developer
during the **2025 UN Women FemTech Hackathon in Beijing**.
Contributed to innovative solutions addressing women's health
challenges in professional environments through collaborative work
with multiple organisations including **Sanicle** and the **NGO
CSW Forum**.

awards.title: Dean's List — Top 5% Academic Performance
awards.awarder: Lincoln University, Faculty of Environment, Society & Design
awards.summary:
Recognised for outstanding academic excellence in **Semester 2,
2024**, placing among the **top 5% of students** in the Faculty
of Environment, Society & Design. Awarded by Lincoln University,
New Zealand's specialist land-based university.

awards.title: Master of Applied Computing with Distinction
awards.summary:
Awarded **Distinction** for achieving an average of **80%+ across
all courses** in the Master of Applied Computing programme at
Lincoln University, New Zealand's specialist land-based university.

awards.title: FemTech Excellence Award — Women's Health Technology Challenge
awards.summary:
Award-winning team member at **China's inaugural FemTech Weekend**
(December 2024). Led product development through intensive
workshops and mentoring sessions, creating innovative solutions
for women's healthcare. This is the same FemTech Weekend
organisation Chan later joined as CTO in March 2025 — the award
preceded and informed the subsequent CTO engagement.

publications.name: Meet the FemTech Founders Redefining Women's Health at Work
publications.summary:
Press feature on Sanicle's founding team and women's-workplace-health
mission, published during the period Chan served as CTO at Sanicle.

publications.name: Weekly Pulse: Remote MCP, Images, Screen Recording
publications.summary:
The MCP server Chan developed (server-google-news / server-google-jobs)
was featured in this PulseMCP newsletter article by Tadas Antanavicius
(https://www.pulsemcp.com/authors/tadas-antanavicius) — third-party
coverage of an early-ecosystem MCP contribution.

publications.name: FemTech Weekend × UN Women CSW69
publications.summary:
Feature article on Chan's UN CSW 69 Speaker presentation — Sanicle AI
for women's workplace health — at the Beyond Beijing 30 Conference,
UN Headquarters NYC, March 14 2025. Article reports IBM
representatives expressed pilot-collaboration interest and the
Minister of Gender and Children's Affairs from Sierra Leone praised
the platform as the first comprehensive solution connecting menstrual
cycle data to actionable workplace policies through AI analysis.

publications.name: Solved Bug: Authentication Redirect Issue in Sanicle-AI Platform
publications.summary:
Community write-up of a Next.js 15 + NextAuth 5 production auth-redirect
bug encountered during the Sanicle-AI build, with the multi-stage solution.
Publicly acknowledged by Vercel Staff (Amy Egan):
"Thanks for sharing your debugging process and solution! This could help
lots of people facing similar auth issues, or trying to avoid them in
the first place."

publications.name: 码农姐妹 — From Math Teacher to Full-Stack Developer
publications.summary:
Guest appearance on the 码农姐妹 (Coder Sisters) podcast,
discussing Chan's career transition from mathematics teaching
to full-stack engineering.

publications.name: Femtech At Work — From Math Teacher to FemTech CTO (Greater China Edition, Ep 21)
publications.summary:
Chan Meng on AI and Women's Health Innovation — Greater China
Edition Episode 21 of the Femtech At Work podcast.

publications.name: Decoding the Future
publications.summary:
Chan-hosted English-language podcast exploring landmark research
papers driving innovation in Artificial Intelligence, LLMs, and
beyond. Companion LinkedIn newsletter ("Decoding the Future") with
800+ subscribers.

publications.name: Future Turing 未来图灵
publications.summary:
Chan-hosted Chinese-language podcast — the 中文 counterpart of
Decoding the Future — focused on decoding landmark research
papers in Artificial Intelligence, LLMs, and other cutting-edge
fields. *"Where is the boundary of intelligence? How will the
future be shaped by code?"*

publications.name: Praxis and Pages 知行书话
publications.summary:
Chan-hosted podcast exploring humanities and social-science
books with the help of NotebookLM — using AI to organise,
distill, and reflect on book content, then sharing those
insights in vivid, engaging conversations.

publications.name: Beyond Thirty 不止三十
publications.summary:
Chan-hosted conversational podcast. As a woman in her 30s, Chan
invites women between the ages of 30 and 40 from all walks of
life for open, authentic conversations. Featured episodes include
EP1 *Don't Do RAG: When Cache-Augmented Generation is All You
Need for Knowledge Tasks*, EP3 *Breaking the illusion of
employment in the age of AI*, EP4 *AI and Future Career Prospects*.

publications.name: Decoding the Future (LinkedIn Newsletter)
publications.summary:
Companion LinkedIn newsletter to the Decoding the Future podcast —
cutting-edge AI research papers and LLM innovations. 800+ tech
enthusiasts subscribed (as of the historical README snapshot).

publications.name: What's Shipping in AI (Weekly AI Digest)
publications.summary:
Every Monday — curated picks from the hottest new AI tools on
Product Hunt. A 3-minute read to keep subscribers in the loop on
what's trending in AI. Free, 5 seconds to subscribe.

references.reference:

Mentorship. I attended Chan's AI programming course. Her insights on
identity and self-awareness deeply inspired me. I also joined her
Office Hours with other sisters, and hearing Chan share her personal
stories and perspectives helped me broaden my way of thinking. I
highly recommend Chan to anyone who wants to grow and advance in the
technology field.

references.name: Amy Li
references.reference:
I am very lucky to have met Chan Meng as my mentor in Forward with
Her 她行. I have connected with her twice, and I have also listened
to a tea party between Chan and her sisters, from which I can feel
a very powerful and subjective spiritual power. Chan's style of
speech is easy-going and humorous, and I am very grateful to her
for sharing her experience and methods unreservedly when she helped
me to answer my questions. She shared her experience and methods,
which allowed me to learn many new channels of information, and
allowed me to clarify my thoughts, and my future path planning
became clearer.

Chan is not only a mentor to me, but also my role model. Her strong
motivation and clear expression and output ability are what I want
to learn, and her rational analysis and answers to specific questions
can also give people a lot of inspiration.

I highly recommend Chan to everyone, you will gain a lot from talking
to her.

references.name: Paige Afanu (Omopeju Afanu)
references.reference:
I had the pleasure of working with Chan Meng on the website and
application development for Sanicle.cloud, where she showcased her
exceptional technical expertise. As a solo developer, she took on
the monumental task of coding the entire website and parts of the
platform from scratch, demonstrating not only her deep technical
skills but also her ability to manage complex projects independently.

Chan's problem-solving abilities and attention to detail are
remarkable. She consistently delivered high-quality work, and her
coding proficiency was evident in every stage of the development
process. She is also incredibly intelligent and driven, always
eager to explore new technologies and methodologies, including the
latest AI tools and AI agents, which speaks to her commitment to
continuous learning and innovation.

Her passion for technology and her ability to tackle challenging
projects make her a valuable asset to any team or initiative. I
highly recommend Chan for any opportunity that requires technical
excellence and a forward-thinking approach to development.

x_brand.relationship: Chief Executive Officer at Sanicle during Chan's CTO tenure (2025–2026); managed Chan directly

references.name: Gabrielle J. Hurst
references.reference:
Chan Meng is an outstanding Senior AI/ML Infrastructure Engineer
whose work consistently combines speed, security, and strategic
foresight.

She delivers complex, high-impact systems with precision and grace.
She's integrated enterprise AI tools like IBM's watsonx, refining
vector databases, or optimizing UX flows for production readiness.

What truly sets Chan apart is her principled approach to software
engineering. She embeds data privacy into the core architecture,
ensuring that client agency, trust, and resilience are never
afterthoughts.

Chan brings structure and calm to ambiguity, advocates for
documentation and transparency, and fosters a culture of
accountability and focus.

Despite operating across time zones, she remains fully present,
responsive, and proactive — turning potential collaboration friction
into a model of asynchronous excellence.

In every sense, Chan is the kind of engineer who elevates those
around her.

Chan is truly the best hand when it comes to workplace health,

I had the pleasure of working alongside Chan, who is not only

She introduced me to the fascinating world of AI programming and
showed me how creating personal web pages could be both simple and

Chan's passion for coding is contagious — working with her made me

references.name: Daryll Hall
references.reference:
I have the pleasure of working with Chan at Sanicle, where she
serves as our Senior AI/ML Infrastructure Engineer — and I can
confidently say she's one of the most talented and reliable
professionals I've worked with.

Chan has done a fantastic job leading the technical development
of our web application, playing a key role in integrating
cutting-edge AI features. Her deep understanding of infrastructure
and AI systems, combined with her hands-on approach, has been
crucial to our progress.

What sets Chan apart is not only her technical expertise but also
her responsiveness and professionalism. She consistently delivers
on modification requests quickly and thoughtfully, ensuring the
product continues to evolve in the right direction. She's always
open to feedback, listens carefully, and adapts with precision
and care.

Beyond her technical contributions, Chan brings a positive,
optimistic energy to every team interaction. She's incredibly
easy to work with — friendly, collaborative, and always looking
for ways to support the team.

Any organization would be lucky to have Chan on board. I can't
recommend her highly enough.
